title: Determine inverse matrices
description: Given a pair of matrices, determine whether they are inverses of each other.

hints:
###The Strategy 

When two matricies are inverses, their product (in either order) equals the identity matrix, $I$. 

Therefore, we must check to see if $AB=I$.

###Finding $AB$ 

$\begin{align}AB&=\left[\begin{array}{rr} 2 & 1
\\ 3 & 6 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{rr} \dfrac{2}{3} & -\dfrac{1}{9}
\\\\-\dfrac{1}{3} & \dfrac{2}{9} \end{array}\right]
\\\\&=\left[\begin{array}{rr} (2\cdot\dfrac{2}{3}+1\cdot-\dfrac{1}{3}) & (2\cdot-\dfrac{1}{9}+1\cdot\dfrac{2}{9})
\\\\(3\cdot\dfrac{2}{3}+6\cdot-\dfrac{1}{3}) & (3\cdot-\dfrac{1}{9}+6\cdot\dfrac{2}{9})\end{array}\right]
\\\\\\&=\left[\begin{array}{rr} 1 & 0
\\ 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]
\\\\&=I\end{align}$ 

Since the product $AB$ gives us the identity matrix, $A$ and $B$ are inverses.

###Summary 

Yes, $A$ and $B$ are inverses.

###The Strategy 

When two matricies are inverses, their product (in either order) equals the identity matrix, $I$. 

Therefore, we must check to see if $AB=I$.

###Finding $AB$ 

$\begin{align}AB&=\left[\begin{array}{rr} 3 & 7
\\ 2 & 1 \end{array}\right]
\left[\begin{array}{rr} -\dfrac{1}{11} & \dfrac{7}{11}
\\\\ \dfrac{2}{11} & -\dfrac{3}{11} \end{array}\right]
\\\\&=\left[\begin{array}{rr} (3\cdot-\dfrac{1}{11}+7\cdot\dfrac{2}{11}) & (3\cdot\dfrac{7}{11}+7\cdot-\dfrac{3}{11})
\\\\(2\cdot-\dfrac{1}{11}+1\cdot\dfrac{2}{11}) & (2\cdot\dfrac{7}{11}+1\cdot-\dfrac{3}{11})\end{array}\right]
\\\\\\&=\left[\begin{array}{rr} 1 & 0
\\ 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]
\\\\&=I\end{align}$ 

Since the product $AB$ gives us the identity matrix, $A$ and $B$ are inverses.

###Summary 

Yes, $A$ and $B$ are inverses.

\\ 6 & 2 \end{array}\right]
\left[\begin{array}{rr} -\dfrac{1}{23} & \dfrac{5}{46}
\\\\\dfrac{3}{23} & \dfrac{4}{23} \end{array}\right]
\\\\&=\left[\begin{array}{rr} (-8\cdot-\dfrac{1}{23}+5\cdot\dfrac{3}{23}) & (-8\cdot\dfrac{5}{46}+5\cdot\dfrac{4}{23})
\\\\(6\cdot-\dfrac{1}{23}+2\cdot\dfrac{3}{23}) & (6\cdot\dfrac{5}{46}+2\cdot\dfrac{4}{23})\end{array}\right]
\\\\\\&=\left[\begin{array}{rr} 1 & 0
\\ 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]
\\\\&=I\end{align}$ 

Since the product $AB$ gives us the identity matrix, $A$ and $B$ are inverses.

\\\\2 & \dfrac{1}{2} \end{array}\right]
\\\\&=\left[\begin{array}{rr} (2\cdot-\dfrac{3}{2}+2\cdot2) & (2\cdot-\dfrac{1}{2}+2\cdot\dfrac{1}{2})
\\\\(-8\cdot-\dfrac{3}{2}-6\cdot2) & (-8\cdot-\dfrac{1}{2}-6\cdot\dfrac{1}{2})\end{array}\right]
\\\\\\&=\left[\begin{array}{rr} 1 & 0

\\\\&=I\end{align}$
